framework.name: Agent Payments Protocol (AP2) Mandate Hierarchy
framework.description: Three-tier cryptographic mandate system: Cart Mandate (human-present, merchant + user signatures), Intent Mandate (human-not-present pre-approval with budget/category/timing constraints), and Payment Mandate (minimal credential derived from the above for authorization flows). All expressed as W3C Verifiable Credentials for tamper resistance and interoperability.
framework.raw_quote: AP2 defines three types of mandates: Cart Mandate ('Human-Present' or 'HP'), where the merchant signs the cart to guarantee fulfillment, and the user signs to approve it. Intent Mandate ('Human-Not-Present' or 'HNP'), where the user pre-approves defined conditions (e.g., budget, product categories, or timing) that authorize an agent to act later. Payment Mandate, which is a minimal credential derived from a Cart or Intent Mandate and appended to the authorization

source.title: Agent Payments Protocol: Building Verifiable Trust for Agentic ...
